title: 10 medicinal constituents of Podophyllum peltatum (mayapple)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Podophyllum peltatum podophyllotoxin is a lignan compound isolated from the plant's rhizome and leaves. It has been found to have antiviral, antibacterial, and anticancer properties, making it a subject of interest in medicine. Specifically, podophyllotoxin has shown promise in treating cervical cancer, acute promyelocytic leukemia, and other diseases. Its unique structure also makes it a valuable tool for understanding the mechanisms of cancer development and progression.

Podophyllum peltatum deoxypodophyllotoxin is a bioactive compound extracted from the plant's rhizome. It has been extensively studied for its antitumor and antiviral properties, showing significant inhibitory effects on cancer cells and viral replication. Deoxypodophyllotoxin has also been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, making it a promising candidate for the development of new medicines against various diseases.

Podophyllum peltatum podocarpic acid is a bioactive compound extracted from its rhizomes and roots. This acidic molecule has been extensively studied for its potential therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and anticancer activities. Its unique structure and functionality have led to the development of various derivatives with improved pharmacological profiles, making it an attractive lead compound for the discovery of novel medicinal agents.

Podophyllum peltatum podophyllotoxol is a highly toxic and powerful alkaloid extracted from the roots of the plant. It has been found to have antitumor properties, particularly in the treatment of cervical cancer, where it inhibits DNA synthesis and prevents cell division. Additionally, podophyllotoxol has been shown to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making it a promising compound for further research and potential applications in medicine.

Podophyllum peltatum cytosine arabinoside is a plant-derived chemotherapy medication used to treat certain types of cancer. It works by interfering with the growth and replication of cancer cells, ultimately causing their death. This medication has been effective in treating acute lymphoblastic leukemia, acute myelogenous leukemia, and other types of blood cancers. Its unique mechanism of action makes it an important tool in the fight against cancer.

Podophyllum peltatum 4-o-methylpodophyllotoxin is a lignan compound extracted from the roots of the plant. It has been extensively studied for its potential therapeutic applications, particularly in cancer treatment. The compound exhibits cytotoxic and antitumor properties, inhibiting the growth of various types of cancer cells while showing low toxicity towards normal cells. Its unique structure and biological activities make it an attractive candidate for further research and development as a potential anticancer agent.

Podophyllum peltatum deoxypodophyllin is a lignan found in various species of Podophyllum plants. It is a bioactive compound extracted from the rhizome and root bark of P. peltatum. Deoxypodophyllin has been studied for its potential anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory properties due to its ability to inhibit cell proliferation and induce apoptosis.

Podophyllum peltatum podophyllotoxine is a toxic and highly carcinogenic compound extracted from the plant's rhizome. It has been studied for its potential anti-cancer properties due to its ability to inhibit cell growth and induce apoptosis in tumor cells. However, its use is limited by its extreme toxicity, which can cause severe side effects and death at even low doses. Further research is needed to fully understand its mechanisms of action and potential therapeutic applications.

Podophyllum peltatum betulinic acid is a type of lignan that has been isolated from the rhizome of this plant. It has been found to possess various biological activities including antimicrobial, antifungal, and anticancer properties. Betulinic acid has also been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, making it a potential treatment for various diseases.

Podophyllum peltatum ursolic acid is a bioactive compound isolated from the rhizomes of P. peltatum. It has been extensively studied for its potential pharmacological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Ursolic acid has also shown promise in preclinical studies as an antitumor agent, with the ability to induce apoptosis and inhibit proliferation of cancer cells.

intro: Podophyllum peltatum has active constituents such as podophyllotoxin and its derivatives, lignans, and flavonoids. These compounds possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of mayapple can help alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis, reduce the risk of cancer recurrence, and even combat viral infections such as herpes simplex. By harnessing the natural power of mayapple's constituents, individuals can potentially improve their overall well-being and quality of life.
intro_desc: Podophyllum peltatum has active constituents such as podophyllotoxin, linoleic acid, and fumaric acid. These constituents possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which can improve lives by reducing pain and inflammation, protecting against oxidative stress, and potentially inhibiting cancer cell growth. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of mayapple may alleviate symptoms of arthritis, skin conditions, and other inflammatory diseases, improving the quality of life for those affected.
